---
title: "Heating and Cooling in UCLCHEM"
jupyter: uclchem_school
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    code-line-numbers: true
    highlight-style: github
    slide-number: true
    scrollable: true
    chalkboard: true
    incremental: true
execute:
  eval: true
  echo: true
---

```{python}

```
## Heating and Cooling in UCLCHEM
This presentation covers using heating and cooling to self-consistently model the temperature evolution in UCLCHEM.

---

## Overview

**Heating processes:**

- Photoelectric heating from PAHs and dust grains
  - Option 1: Bakes & Tielens (1994) 
  - Option 2: Weingartner & Draine (2001)
- $\mathrm{H}_2$ formation
- $\mathrm{H}_2$ photodissociation
- $\mathrm{H}_2$ ultraviolet pumping
- Carbon ionization heating
- $\mathrm{H}_2$ Cosmic ray heating
- Chemical reaction enthalpy (optional, disabled )
- Turbulent heating
- Gas grain collisional heating

---

## Overview

**Cooling processes:**

- Line cooling ($\mathrm{H, C^+, O, C, CO,p-H2, o-H2}$)
- Atomic fine structure cooling
- Continuum cooling from dust
- $\mathrm{H}_2$ Vibrational
- Gas grain collisional cooling


---

Then we can compute:
$$ \frac{\mathrm{d}T_{\rm gas}}{\mathrm{d}t} = \frac{\gamma -1}{k_\mathrm{B} n_{\rm H,nuclei}}\left(\sum_m \Gamma_m - \sum_m \Lambda_m \right)
$$
where $\Gamma_m$ and $\Lambda_m$ are the heating and cooling rates for each process $m$. They depend on many different physical conditions:
```{fortran}
heatingValues(2) = photoelectricHeatingWeingartner(gasTemperature,gasDensity,habingField,abundances(nelec),metallicity)
``` 

# Preliminary plots from default models
---
### Default heating and cooling effects:
![](figures/figure_b_high_rad_zeta.png){width=80%}

### Gas phase temperature evolution:
![](figures/figure_b_eq_comparison-1.png){width=80%}

### Dust phase tempereatures:
![](figures/figure_b_dust_temp.png){width=80%}

### Dominant heating and cooling processes:
![](figures/figure_b_dominant.png){width=80%}


# Part 1 — Basic Usage

---

## Imports

```{python}
import uclchem
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
```

---

## Example 1: Basic Model with Heating Enabled

Let's start with a simple cloud model with heating and cooling enabled (the default behavior). We'll model a molecular cloud core at constant density.

```{python}
param_dict_heating = {
    "initialDens": 1e4,  # Initial density (cm^-3)
    "initialTemp": 10.0,  # Initial temperature (K)
    "finalTime": 1.0e6,  # Final time (years)
    "baseAv": 10.0,  # Visual extinction
    "rout": 0.1,  # Outer radius (pc)
    "freefall": False,
    "heatingFlag": True,  # Enable heating (default)
}

cloud = uclchem.model.Cloud(param_dict=param_dict_heating)

physics, abundances = cloud.get_dataframes(joined=False)
_, _, rates = cloud.get_dataframes(joined=False, with_rates=True)
_, _, heating = cloud.get_dataframes(joined=False, with_heating=True)
```

---

## Example 1: Visualizing Temperature Evolution

The `physics` DataFrame contains physical properties including gas temperature.

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
ax.plot(physics["Time"], physics["gasTemp"], linewidth=2, color="darkred")
ax.set_xlabel("Time (years)", fontsize=12)
ax.set_ylabel("Gas Temperature (K)", fontsize=12)
ax.set_xscale("log")
ax.set_title("Gas Temperature Evolution with Heating/Cooling", fontsize=14)
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

---

## Example 2: Comparing Heating On vs Off

To see the impact of heating/cooling processes, let's run two models side by side.

```{python}
param_dict_no_heating = param_dict_heating.copy()
param_dict_no_heating["heatingFlag"] = False

cloud_no_heat = uclchem.model.Cloud(param_dict=param_dict_no_heating)

physics_no_heat, abundances_no_heat = cloud_no_heat.get_dataframes(joined=False)
_, _, heating_no_heat = cloud_no_heat.get_dataframes(joined=False, with_heating=True)
```

---

## Example 2: Results

```{python}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))

axes[0].plot(physics["Time"], physics["gasTemp"], label="Heating ON", linewidth=2)
axes[0].plot(physics_no_heat["Time"], physics_no_heat["gasTemp"],
             label="Heating OFF", linewidth=2, linestyle="--")
axes[0].set_xlabel("Time (years)", fontsize=12)
axes[0].set_ylabel("Gas Temperature (K)", fontsize=12)
axes[0].set_xscale("log")
axes[0].set_title("Temperature Evolution", fontsize=14)
axes[0].legend()
axes[0].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

axes[1].plot(physics["Time"], abundances["H2O"], label="Heating ON", linewidth=2)
axes[1].plot(physics_no_heat["Time"], abundances_no_heat["H2O"],
             label="Heating OFF", linewidth=2, linestyle="--")
axes[1].set_xlabel("Time (years)", fontsize=12)
axes[1].set_ylabel("H$_2$O Abundance", fontsize=12)
axes[1].set_xscale("log")
axes[1].set_yscale("log")
axes[1].set_title("H₂O Abundance Evolution", fontsize=14)
axes[1].legend()
axes[1].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

---

## Example 3: Accessing Heating and Cooling Rates

The `heating` DataFrame includes individual contributions from each process.

```{python}
heating_cols = [col for col in heating.columns if "heating" in col.lower() and col != "Time"]
cooling_cols = [col for col in heating.columns if "cooling" in col.lower()]

print(f"Found {len(heating_cols)} heating processes")
print(f"Found {len(cooling_cols)} cooling processes")
```

---

## Example 3: Plotting Individual Contributions

```{python}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(12, 10))
time = physics["Time"]

for col in heating_cols:
    if heating[col].abs().max() > 1e-30:
        label = col.replace("_heating", "").replace("_", " ")
        axes[0].plot(time, heating[col], label=label, linewidth=2, alpha=0.7)

axes[0].set_xlabel("Time (years)", fontsize=12)
axes[0].set_ylabel("Heating Rate (erg cm⁻³ s⁻¹)", fontsize=12)
axes[0].set_xscale("log")
axes[0].set_yscale("symlog", linthresh=1e-30)
axes[0].set_title("Heating Processes", fontsize=14)
axes[0].legend(fontsize=8, loc="best")
axes[0].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

for col in cooling_cols:
    if heating[col].abs().max() > 1e-40:
        label = col.replace("_cooling", "").replace("_", " ")
        axes[1].plot(time, heating[col], label=label, linewidth=2, alpha=0.7)

axes[1].set_xlabel("Time (years)", fontsize=12)
axes[1].set_ylabel("Cooling Rate (erg cm⁻³ s⁻¹)", fontsize=12)
axes[1].set_xscale("log")
axes[1].set_yscale("symlog", linthresh=1e-30)
axes[1].set_title("Major Cooling Processes", fontsize=14)
axes[1].legend(fontsize=8, loc="best")
axes[1].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

---

## Example 4: Writing Heating Rates to a File

Use the `heatingFile` parameter to save detailed heating/cooling contributions to disk.

```{python}
# Uncomment to run a model to disk:

# param_dict_with_file = param_dict_heating.copy()
# param_dict_with_file["outputFile"] = "heating_demo.dat"
# param_dict_with_file["heatingFile"] = "heating_rates.dat"

# result = uclchem.model.cloud(
#     param_dict=param_dict_with_file, out_species=["CO", "H2O", "OH"]
# )
```

---

## Example 5: Different Physical Conditions

Compare a dense, cold core with a less dense, warmer environment.

```{python}
param_cold_core = {
    "initialDens": 1e5, "initialTemp": 10.0, "finalTime": 1.0e6,
    "baseAv": 20.0, "rout": 0.05, "freefall": False, "heatingFlag": True,
}

param_warm_cloud = {
    "initialDens": 1e3, "initialTemp": 30.0, "finalTime": 1.0e6,
    "baseAv": 5.0, "rout": 0.5, "freefall": False, "heatingFlag": True,
}

cloud_cold = uclchem.model.Cloud(param_dict=param_cold_core)
phys_cold, abund_cold = cloud_cold.get_dataframes(joined=False)

cloud_warm = uclchem.model.Cloud(param_dict=param_warm_cloud)
phys_warm, abund_warm = cloud_warm.get_dataframes(joined=False)
```

---

## Example 5: Results

```{python}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))

axes[0].plot(phys_cold["Time"], phys_cold["gasTemp"],
             label=f"Dense Core (n=1e5)", linewidth=2)
axes[0].plot(phys_warm["Time"], phys_warm["gasTemp"],
             label=f"Diffuse Cloud (n=1e3)", linewidth=2)
axes[0].set_xlabel("Time (years)", fontsize=12)
axes[0].set_ylabel("Gas Temperature (K)", fontsize=12)
axes[0].set_xscale("log")
axes[0].set_title("Temperature Evolution", fontsize=14)
axes[0].legend()
axes[0].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

axes[1].plot(phys_cold["Time"], abund_cold["CO"], label="Dense Core", linewidth=2)
axes[1].plot(phys_warm["Time"], abund_warm["CO"], label="Diffuse Cloud", linewidth=2)
axes[1].set_xlabel("Time (years)", fontsize=12)
axes[1].set_ylabel("CO Abundance", fontsize=12)
axes[1].set_xscale("log")
axes[1].set_yscale("log")
axes[1].set_title("CO Abundance", fontsize=14)
axes[1].legend()
axes[1].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```


---

## Summary: Key Parameters

| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| `heatingFlag` | Enable/disable all heating (default: `True`) |
| `heatingFile` | Path to write detailed rates |
| `initialTemp` | Starting gas temperature |
| `baseAv` | Visual extinction (controls UV attenuation) |
| `zeta` | Cosmic ray ionization rate |

---

## Advanced: Chemical Reaction Enthalpy

UCLCHEM can optionally include enthalpy changes from chemical reactions. Configure via `user_settings.yaml` before rebuilding:

```yaml
# In Makerates/user_settings.yaml
add_delta_enthalpy: GAS   # False | GAS | ALL
```

Then rebuild:

```bash
cd Makerates
python MakeRates.py
cd ..
pip install .
```

---

# Part 2 — Controlling Individual Mechanisms

Now we continue to cover enabling and disabling specific heating and cooling mechanisms in UCLCHEM.

---

## Imports

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import uclchem
from uclchem import advanced
```

---

## Step 1: Run Baseline Model with All Mechanisms

```{python}
param_dict = {
    "initialDens": 1e4,
    "initialTemp": 10.0,
    "finalTime": 1.0e6,
    "baseAv": 10.0,
    "rout": 0.1,
    "freefall": False,
    "heatingFlag": True,
}

cloud_full = uclchem.model.Cloud(param_dict=param_dict)

physics_full, abundances_full, rates_full, heating_full = cloud_full.get_dataframes(
    joined=False, with_rates=True, with_heating=True
)

print(f"Temperature range: {physics_full['gasTemp'].min():.2f} - {physics_full['gasTemp'].max():.2f} K")
print(f"Final temperature: {physics_full['gasTemp'].iloc[-1]:.2f} K")
```

---

## Step 2: Identify the Most Important Processes

```{python}
heating_cols = [col for col in heating_full.columns if col.endswith("Heating")]
cooling_cols = [col for col in heating_full.columns if col.endswith("Cooling")]

heating_importance = {col: heating_full[col].abs().sum() for col in heating_cols}
cooling_importance = {col: heating_full[col].abs().sum() for col in cooling_cols}

heating_sorted = sorted(heating_importance.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
cooling_sorted = sorted(cooling_importance.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)

print("TOP 5 HEATING PROCESSES")
for i, (process, contribution) in enumerate(heating_sorted[:5], 1):
    print(f"{i}. {process.replace('Heating_', ''):40s} {contribution:.2e}")

print("\nTOP 5 COOLING PROCESSES")
for i, (process, contribution) in enumerate(cooling_sorted[:5], 1):
    print(f"{i}. {process.replace('Cooling_', ''):40s} {contribution:.2e}")
```

---

## Step 3: Visualize the Dominant Processes

```{python}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(12, 10))
time = heating_full["Time"]

for process, _ in heating_sorted[:5]:
    label = process.replace("Heating_", "")
    axes[0].plot(time, heating_full[process], label=label, linewidth=2, alpha=0.8)

axes[0].set_xlabel("Time (years)", fontsize=12)
axes[0].set_ylabel("Heating Rate (erg cm⁻³ s⁻¹)", fontsize=12)
axes[0].set_xscale("log")
axes[0].set_yscale("symlog", linthresh=1e-30)
axes[0].set_title("Top 5 Heating Processes", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold")
axes[0].legend(fontsize=10)
axes[0].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

for process, _ in cooling_sorted[:5]:
    label = process.replace("Cooling_", "").replace("LineCooling_", "")
    axes[1].plot(time, heating_full[process], label=label, linewidth=2, alpha=0.8)

axes[1].set_xlabel("Time (years)", fontsize=12)
axes[1].set_ylabel("Cooling Rate (erg cm⁻³ s⁻¹)", fontsize=12)
axes[1].set_xscale("log")
axes[1].set_yscale("symlog", linthresh=1e-30)
axes[1].set_title("Top 5 Cooling Processes", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold")
axes[1].legend(fontsize=10)
axes[1].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

---

## Step 4: Introduction to HeatingSettings

The `HeatingSettings` class allows you to control individual mechanisms.

```{python}
settings = advanced.HeatingSettings()

heating_mechanisms = settings.get_heating_modules()
cooling_mechanisms = settings.get_cooling_modules()

print("AVAILABLE HEATING MECHANISMS:")
for mech, active in heating_mechanisms.items():
    status = "ACTIVE" if active else "INACTIVE"
    print(f"  {mech:40s} {status}")

print("\nAVAILABLE COOLING MECHANISMS:")
for mech, active in cooling_mechanisms.items():
    status = "ACTIVE" if active else "INACTIVE"
    print(f"  {mech:40s} {status}")
```

---

## Step 5: Disable the Most Important Mechanisms

```{python}
# Disable H2 formation heating (often important)
settings.set_heating_mechanism(settings.H2_FORMATION, False)
print("Disabled: H2 Formation Heating")

# Disable line cooling (dominant cooling in molecular clouds)
settings.set_cooling_mechanism(settings.MOLECULAR_LINE_COOLING, False)
print("Disabled: Molecular Line Cooling")
```

---

## Step 6: Run Model with Disabled Mechanisms

```{python}
cloud_limited = uclchem.model.Cloud(param_dict=param_dict)

physics_limited, abundances_limited, rates_limited, heating_limited = (
    cloud_limited.get_dataframes(joined=False, with_rates=True, with_heating=True)
)

print(f"Temperature range: {physics_limited['gasTemp'].min():.2f} - {physics_limited['gasTemp'].max():.2f} K")
print(f"Final temperature: {physics_limited['gasTemp'].iloc[-1]:.2f} K")

settings.reset_to_defaults()
print("\nSettings reset to defaults.")
```

---

## Step 7: Compare Temperature Evolution

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 6))

ax.plot(physics_full["Time"], physics_full["gasTemp"],
        label="All Mechanisms Active", linewidth=2.5, color="darkblue")
ax.plot(physics_limited["Time"], physics_limited["gasTemp"],
        label="Key Mechanisms Disabled", linewidth=2.5, color="darkred", linestyle="--")

ax.set_xlabel("Time (years)", fontsize=12)
ax.set_ylabel("Gas Temperature (K)", fontsize=12)
ax.set_xscale("log")
ax.set_title("Temperature Evolution: Impact of Disabling Key Mechanisms",
             fontsize=14, fontweight="bold")
ax.legend(fontsize=11)
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

---

## Step 8: Compare Chemical Abundances

```{python}
key_species = ["CO", "H2O", "OH", "NH3"]
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(14, 10))
axes = axes.flatten()

for idx, species in enumerate(key_species):
    if species in abundances_full.columns and species in abundances_limited.columns:
        axes[idx].plot(physics_full["Time"], abundances_full[species],
                       label="All Mechanisms", linewidth=2.5, color="darkblue")
        axes[idx].plot(physics_full["Time"], abundances_limited[species],
                       label="Key Mechanisms Disabled", linewidth=2.5,
                       color="darkred", linestyle="--")
        axes[idx].set_xlabel("Time (years)", fontsize=11)
        axes[idx].set_ylabel("Abundance", fontsize=11)
        axes[idx].set_xscale("log")
        axes[idx].set_yscale("log")
        axes[idx].set_title(f"{species} Abundance", fontsize=13, fontweight="bold")
        axes[idx].legend(fontsize=9)
        axes[idx].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

---

## Step 9: Compare Total Heating and Cooling Rates

```{python}
total_heating_full = heating_full[[c for c in heating_full.columns if c.endswith("Heating")]].sum(axis=1)
total_cooling_full = heating_full[[c for c in heating_full.columns if c.endswith("Cooling")]].sum(axis=1)
total_heating_limited = heating_limited[[c for c in heating_limited.columns if c.endswith("Heating")]].sum(axis=1)
total_cooling_limited = heating_limited[[c for c in heating_limited.columns if c.endswith("Cooling")]].sum(axis=1)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(12, 10))

axes[0].plot(physics_full["Time"], total_heating_full,
             label="All Mechanisms", linewidth=2.5, color="darkred")
axes[0].plot(physics_limited["Time"], total_heating_limited,
             label="Key Mechanisms Disabled", linewidth=2.5, color="orange", linestyle="--")
axes[0].set_xlabel("Time (years)", fontsize=12)
axes[0].set_ylabel("Total Heating Rate (erg cm⁻³ s⁻¹)", fontsize=12)
axes[0].set_xscale("log")
axes[0].set_yscale("symlog", linthresh=1e-30)
axes[0].set_title("Total Heating Rate Comparison", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold")
axes[0].legend(fontsize=11)
axes[0].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

axes[1].plot(physics_full["Time"], total_cooling_full,
             label="All Mechanisms", linewidth=2.5, color="darkblue")
axes[1].plot(physics_limited["Time"], total_cooling_limited,
             label="Key Mechanisms Disabled", linewidth=2.5, color="cyan", linestyle="--")
axes[1].set_xlabel("Time (years)", fontsize=12)
axes[1].set_ylabel("Total Cooling Rate (erg cm⁻³ s⁻¹)", fontsize=12)
axes[1].set_xscale("log")
axes[1].set_yscale("symlog", linthresh=1e-30)
axes[1].set_title("Total Cooling Rate Comparison", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold")
axes[1].legend(fontsize=11)
axes[1].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

---

## Summary

::: {.incremental}
1. Run a **baseline model** with all mechanisms enabled
2. **Analyze** which processes dominate (integrated contribution)
3. **Disable specific mechanisms** using `HeatingSettings`
4. **Compare models** to quantify the impact
:::

---

## HeatingSettings Reference

**Methods:**

- `get_heating_modules()` — list all heating mechanisms and status
- `get_cooling_modules()` — list all cooling mechanisms and status
- `set_heating_mechanism(mechanism, active)` — enable/disable
- `set_cooling_mechanism(mechanism, active)` — enable/disable
- `reset_to_defaults()` — reset all to defaults
